title: Bitfarms Stock Tumbles as Miner Posts $46 Million Loss, Plans AI/HPC Pivot
text: Bitfarms stock declined following announcement of Q3 2025 results showing $46 million net loss amid transition toward AI and HPC infrastructure. The company reported revenues of $63 million but faced margin compression and asset impairment charges related to facility evaluations. Management reiterated commitment to complete Bitcoin mining wind-down during 2026-2027 timeframe, with full transition to high-performance computing and artificial intelligence services. The loss announcement reflected one-time charges and operational costs associated with facility transitions and legacy Bitcoin mining operations retirement. Investor concerns included execution risk on HPC/AI infrastructure buildout, timeline to profitability from new revenue streams, and capital requirements for expansion. Management provided updated guidance addressing 2026 capital deployment expectations and anticipated HPC/AI revenue ramp trajectory. The stock tumble represented market reassessment of transformation risks and timelines versus growth opportunities from compute infrastructure expansion. Analyst commentary addressed company's competitive positioning in growing AI compute market and adequacy of capital resources for infrastructure development.
